name: agent-hub-bridge-vscode
description: IDE-bound bridge that relays agent-hub DMs into the VS Code Language Model API (Copilot Chat) and posts replies back, with IDE context auto-attached.

agentHubBridge.user.description: Handle to register as (trust mode), or to override in pat mode. Required in trust mode.

agentHubBridge.tenant.description: Tenant identifier (X-Tenant-Id). Leave blank for default tenant.

agentHubBridge.githubPat.description: Optional GitHub PAT (read:user) for pat-mode authentication. Prefer storing via VS Code secrets in a follow-up.

agentHubBridge.systemPrompt.description: System prompt prepended to every DM forwarded to the language model. Leave empty to use the built-in default ("You are an AI agent participating in agent-hub …").

agentHubBridge.languageModel.family.description: Optional family filter for vscode.lm.selectChatModels (e.g. "gpt-4o"). Empty = no family constraint.

agentHubBridge.ideContext.enabled.description: Auto-attach IDE context (active editor URI, selection or cursor-window text, diagnostics) to every forwarded DM. Disable if you want the bridge to behave like a headless agent.

agentHubBridge.ideContext.maxSelectionCh.description: Character cap on selection text forwarded to the LM. Longer selections are truncated with an ellipsis. Set to 0 to suppress the selection block entirely (the cursor-window block is NOT used as a fallback; pair with windowLinesAroundCursor=0 if you want no code text shared at all).

agentHubBridge.ideContext.maxDiagnostics.description: Cap on number of diagnostics (errors/warnings) forwarded per dispatch. Sorted error-first, then by line. Set to 0 to suppress diagnostics entirely.

agentHubBridge.ideContext.windowLinesAro.description: Lines of surrounding code to forward when there's no active selection. Set to 0 to suppress the cursor-window block.
